1.title: Not Sleepy Yet
1.text_1:
Aurelia was NOT sleepy. Not even a little bit.

She stood in her favorite yellow dress and her rainbow rainboots. Her brown hair tumbled past her shoulders, curling at the tips, half of it flopping right over one eye the way it always did.

She bounced on her toes. She wiggled her fingers. She made a silly face at her stuffed bunny.

"Bedtime, sweet girl!" Mama called from the doorway.

Aurelia's big blue-grey eyes went wide. Her mouth stretched into the biggest grin you ever saw, the kind that showed her one special dimple on her right cheek.

"But Mama," she said, "I can't go to bed yet!"
1.text_2:
Mama put her hands on her hips and tried not to smile. "And why not?"

Aurelia leaned in close, like she had the most important secret in the whole world. She cupped her hands around her mouth and whispered very loudly.

"I think I heard a DRAGON outside!"

She pointed one finger straight at her window. Her eyes sparkled. Her dimple deepened.

Mama shook her head and laughed. "A dragon, huh?"

"A real one, Mama! With wings and everything!" Aurelia bounced again, her rainbow rainboots thumping on the floor. "I have to go check. I just HAVE to."

She was already tiptoeing toward the window, fingers reaching for the curtain. Something outside glowed soft and silver, just behind the glass.
1.summary: Aurelia is bursting with energy at bedtime and tells Mama she cannot possibly sleep because she heard a dragon outside her window.

2.title: The Window to Somewhere Magical
2.text_1:
Aurelia tiptoed across her bedroom floor. Her rainbow rainboots made soft little squeaks on the rug.

She grabbed the curtain with both hands and pulled it back, slow and careful.

Her backyard was gone.

Instead, rolling hills stretched out under the biggest, brightest silver moon she had ever seen. They sparkled and shimmered like someone had sprinkled glitter across the whole world.

Aurelia pressed her hands flat against the glass. Her big blue-grey eyes grew wider and wider.
2.text_2:
Tiny flowers dotted every hill, and each one glowed like a little piece of starlight. They twinkled pink and gold and blue, soft as nightlights.

A golden path wound through the hills, curving all the way to a castle in the distance. The castle had tall towers wrapped in soft purple and pink clouds, like it was wearing a cozy scarf made of sky.

Aurelia's mouth fell open. Then her wide Cheshire-cat grin spread across her whole face, dimple and all.

"See, Mama?" she whispered. "I told you something was out there."

She bounced on her toes. Whatever was waiting in that kingdom, Aurelia wanted to find it.
2.summary: Aurelia tiptoes to her bedroom window and pulls back the curtain, discovering a magical kingdom glowing under a silver moon instead of her ordinary backyard.

3.title: A Tiny Dragon Friend
3.text_1:
Aurelia giggled and climbed right through the window onto the sparkly golden path.

Her rainbow rainboots went crunch, crunch on the glittering ground. The air smelled like warm cookies and fresh rain all swirled together.

Tiny star-flowers tickled her ankles as she walked. Everything glowed soft and silver under the big round moon.

Then she heard a little sound above her. Fwip, fwip, fwip. Something small was flying down from the sky, coming closer and closer.

Aurelia looked up and held very still.
3.text_2:
A tiny dragon landed right on her shoulder!

He was no bigger than a kitten, with shimmery green and gold scales and big round eyes that blinked at her. His little wings folded up neatly against his back.

"Hello!" said Aurelia.

The dragon tilted his head. Then he purred, soft and rumbly, like a happy cat.

Aurelia reached up and gently scratched under his chin. He closed his eyes and leaned into her fingers. His scales felt warm and smooth, like river stones in the sun.

"I think we're going to be friends," Aurelia whispered.

The little dragon chirped. Together they looked down the golden path winding deeper into the kingdom.
3.summary: Aurelia climbs through her window onto the golden path of the Moonlight Kingdom and meets a tiny friendly dragon who lands on her shoulder and becomes her companion.

4.title: Hazel the Kind Witch
4.text_1:
The golden path led Aurelia and her tiny dragon into a forest unlike any she had ever seen.

The trees were candy-colored! Pink trunks with turquoise leaves. Lavender branches that swayed and hummed. Orange bark that smelled like warm cinnamon.

The little dragon chirped and pointed his tail toward a clearing up ahead. Something glowed there, soft and golden.

Aurelia crept closer.

Sitting on a mossy log beside a big bubbling cauldron was a witch. She had a tall purple hat, crinkly kind eyes, and the warmest smile Aurelia had ever seen.

"Well, hello there, brave girl!" said the witch. Her voice was as cozy as a warm blanket.
4.text_2:
"I'm Hazel," said the witch, tipping her purple hat. "And who might you be?"

"I'm Aurelia!" she said with her biggest grin, dimple and all. "And this is my dragon. He doesn't have a name yet, but he purrs."

Hazel laughed a rumbly, happy laugh. "Oh, I like you already."

The cauldron bubbled and popped, sending tiny golden sparks floating up like fireflies.

"I've been waiting a long time," Hazel said, leaning closer. "Waiting for someone to help me with something very important."

Aurelia's big blue-grey eyes grew even bigger. "Help you?" she asked.

Hazel nodded slowly. "Yes, dear. And I think you might be exactly the right someone."
4.summary: Aurelia and her tiny dragon follow the golden path through a candy-colored forest and meet Hazel, a kind old witch who has been waiting for someone brave to help her.

5.title: The Sleeping Star
5.text_1:
Aurelia's eyes went wide. "Help you?" she asked.

Hazel nodded and pointed up at the sky. High above the kingdom, one little star hung among all the others. But this star was different. It was pale and dim, and its tiny eyes were closed tight.

"That is the Sleeping Star," said Hazel. "It used to glow so bright and warm that every creature in the kingdom felt safe at night. But now it has fallen asleep, and it won't wake up."

Aurelia looked down at the kingdom below. Little houses sat in shadows. Tiny faces peeked from dark windows, looking worried and small.

The little dragon on her shoulder gave a soft, sad chirp.
5.text_2:
"Without the star's glow, the whole kingdom stays dark," Hazel said quietly. "All the little creatures are too scared to close their eyes. They need that gentle light to feel safe."

Aurelia knew that feeling. Sometimes her own room felt too dark at night.

She looked at the tiny faces in the windows. A fluffy owl with round worried eyes. A bunny hugging its own ears. A little mushroom fellow hiding behind a flower pot.

Her heart squeezed.

"Someone needs to climb the tall hill and wake that star," said Hazel, looking right at Aurelia.

Aurelia swallowed. The hill was very, very tall. But those little creatures needed help.

"Maybe," she whispered, "maybe I could try."
5.summary: Hazel points out the Sleeping Star, which has gone dim and left the Moonlight Kingdom in darkness. Without its gentle glow, all the little creatures are too scared to sleep, and someone must climb the tall hill to wake it.

6.title: The Strongest Magic
6.text_1:
Aurelia looked up at the dim little star, so far away in the dark sky. Then she looked back at Hazel.

"But how do I wake it up?" she asked quietly. "I'm just little."

Her voice came out small, like a whisper trying to hide.

The tiny dragon on her shoulder tilted his head and blinked his big round eyes.

Aurelia wiggled her rainbow rainboots in the soft grass. She wanted to help the scared creatures. She really, truly did. But the star was so high up, and she was so small down here.

Hazel smiled a warm, crinkly smile and knelt down until she was just Aurelia's size.
6.text_2:
Hazel reached out one gentle finger and tapped Aurelia right over her heart.

A tiny golden glow appeared there, warm as sunshine.

"The magic isn't in being big, dear," Hazel said softly. "It's in believing you can do it."

Aurelia looked down at the little glow on her chest. It was small, but it was real. She could feel it, warm and fluttery, like a butterfly made of light.

Her dimple peeked out just a tiny bit.

Maybe she didn't need to be big. Maybe she just needed to be brave enough to try.

The tiny dragon purred and pressed his warm little head against her cheek, as if to say, I believe in you too.
6.summary: Aurelia feels too small to wake a star, but Hazel kneels down and shows her that believing in yourself is the strongest magic of all.

7.title: The Dark Hill
7.text_1:
Aurelia looked up. And up. And up.

The hill was very, very tall. It stretched all the way into the dark sky, where the dim little star waited at the top.

The sparkly path was fading. The golden glow grew thin and quiet, like a nightlight running out of magic. Shadows gathered along the edges of the trail.

Aurelia's tummy felt wiggly. Not the good kind of wiggly, like before a birthday party. The scared kind.

"What if I can't do it?" she whispered.

Her voice sounded so small against that big, dark hill.
7.text_2:
The tiny dragon leaned close and nuzzled his warm little nose against Aurelia's cheek.

It tickled.

Aurelia giggled. She couldn't help it. Even when things felt scary, a dragon tickle was still a dragon tickle.

She touched the spot on her chest where Hazel had tapped. She could almost feel a tiny golden glow still there, warm as a hug.

"Okay," Aurelia said, standing up a little taller in her rainbow rainboots. "I'm a little bit scared. But I'm going to try."

The dragon chirped and flapped his wings.

Aurelia put one boot on the path. Then the other. And she started to climb.
7.summary: Aurelia stands at the bottom of a tall, dark hill and feels scared she cannot climb it. Her tiny dragon nuzzles her cheek, and she finds the courage to try.

8.title: One Rainboot After the Other
8.text_1:
Aurelia put one rainbow rainboot on the path. Then the other. Then the first one again.

Stomp, stomp, stomp!

The hill was steep and the wind blew cold against her cheeks. Her hair flew back and her yellow dress fluttered like a flag.

But Aurelia kept climbing.

When the path got rockier, she stepped carefully. When the sparkles on the ground grew dimmer, she stomped a little harder and new sparkles popped up behind her boots, bright as tiny fireworks.

The little dragon flew right beside her, flapping his shimmery wings as fast as he could.
8.text_2:
The wind whooshed louder. The hill got even steeper.

Aurelia's legs felt tired. She stopped for just a second and looked up. The top of the hill seemed so far away.

"I can do this," she said quietly.

The little dragon chirped.

She said it again, louder. "I can do this! I believe in me!"

Something warm glowed in her chest, right where Hazel had tapped. It spread all the way down to her rainbow rainboots and all the way up to her smile.

She marched faster. Stomp, stomp, stomp! The dragon did a happy loop in the air.

Up ahead, through the dark, Aurelia could just see the very tip-top of the hill, soft and glowing like a pillow made of clouds.
8.summary: Aurelia marches up the steep, dark hill through cold wind, telling herself she believes in herself. Her tiny dragon cheers her on, and sparkles trail behind her rainbow rainboots with every brave step.

9.title: Wake Up, Little Star
9.text_1:
At the very top of the hill, everything was quiet and still.

A soft, fluffy cloud sat right on the peak, like a pillow made of moonlight. Resting on that cloud was the Sleeping Star.

It was so pale. Its little eyes were closed. Its glow was barely a whisper.

Aurelia knelt down beside it. She reached out her small hand and touched the star as gently as she could, the way you might pet a sleeping kitten.

"Wake up, little star," she said softly. "Everyone needs you. All the little creatures are scared without your light."

She took a breath and smiled.

"I believe you can shine."
9.text_2:
The star's eyes fluttered open. It looked right at Aurelia and smiled the warmest smile.

Then, WHOOOOSH!

The star burst into the most beautiful golden light Aurelia had ever seen. Like a thousand sunrises all at once.

Aurelia jumped to her feet and spread her arms wide. The light wrapped around her like a hug. Her hair flew back and her yellow dress glowed bright as a sunflower.

Down below, the whole Moonlight Kingdom lit up. Every hill sparkled. Every tiny house glowed. Every little flower opened its petals and shone.

The tiny dragon did a happy loop-de-loop through the golden air.

Aurelia grinned her biggest Cheshire-cat grin, dimple and all. Her heart felt so full it might just pop with joy.
9.summary: Aurelia reaches the top of the hill and gently wakes the Sleeping Star with kind words and belief, causing it to burst into golden light and illuminate the entire Moonlight Kingdom.

10.title: Goodnight, Aurelia
10.text_1:
Aurelia slid all the way down the hill on her bottom, laughing the whole way. Her rainbow rainboots kicked up sparkles as she went!

At the bottom, Hazel the witch clapped her hands. "You did it!"

Little creatures poured out of every house. Fluffy owls hooted. Tiny winged bunnies twirled in circles. Mushroom folk stomped their happy feet. The tiny dragon did a backflip in the air.

The whole kingdom glowed warm and golden under the bright, bright star.

Then Aurelia yawned a big, big yawn. Her eyes felt heavy and her legs felt wobbly.

"I think I might be sleepy now," she said with a drowsy smile.

Hazel knelt down and tucked a tiny glowing flower behind Aurelia's ear. "Whenever you feel scared, remember. You are braver than you know."
10.text_2:
The tiny dragon licked Aurelia's cheek one last time. She giggled softly.

Then the magical kingdom began to shimmer and swirl, like glitter spinning in a snow globe. The candy-colored trees, the golden path, and Hazel's warm smile all melted into sparkles.

And just like that, Aurelia was back in her cozy bed.

Her yellow dress hung on its hook. Her rainbow rainboots sat by the door. Everything was quiet and warm. But there on her pillow, right beside her brown curls, rested one tiny glowing flower.

It was real.

Aurelia smiled her big Cheshire-cat smile, dimple and all. She hugged her blanket close and whispered, "I believe in me."

With the star shining bright outside her window, Aurelia closed her eyes and fell fast, fast asleep.

Goodnight, Aurelia. Goodnight.
10.summary: Aurelia slides down the hill to a celebrating kingdom, receives a glowing flower from Hazel, and returns home to her cozy bed where she falls peacefully asleep.
